channel.title: NPR Topics: Science
channel.description: The latest health and science news. Updates on medicine, healthy living, nutrition, drugs, diet, and advances in science and technology. Subscribe to the Health & Science podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: NASA's Artemis II crew readies for Monday's lunar flyby. Here's what you need to know
item.description: At its closest point, the crew of Artemis II will loop about 4,000 miles from the lunar surface late Monday. The astronauts will also venture farther into space than any previous human mission.
item.content:encoded:
At its closest point, the crew of Artemis II will loop about 4,000 miles from the lunar surface late Monday. The astronauts will also venture farther into space than any previous human mission.

(Image credit: NASA via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: These rock-climbing fish can shimmy up a 50-foot waterfall
item.description: New research from the Democratic Republic of Congo offers a behavioral and anatomical portrait of a species that can achieve surprising athletic feats.
item.content:encoded:
New research from the Democratic Republic of Congo offers a behavioral and anatomical portrait of a species that can achieve surprising athletic feats.

(Image credit: Pacifique Kiwele Mutambala)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: NASA's Artemis II crew are quite the photographers. See what they've snapped so far
item.description: Many of the photos that have come out of the moon mission so far were taken by crew members. NASA says the crew is getting guidance from scientists on what to capture when they get closer to the moon.
item.content:encoded:
Many of the photos that have come out of the moon mission so far were taken by crew members. NASA says the crew is getting guidance from scientists on what to capture when they get closer to the moon.

(Image credit: Reid Wiseman)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: Big tech's next move is to put data centers in space. Can it work?
item.description: In orbit, power is free. But everything else is expensive.
item.content:encoded: In orbit, power is free. But everything else is expensive.
item.dc:creator: Geoff Brumfiel

item.title: NASA's Artemis II has left Earth's orbit, and 4 astronauts now head to the moon
item.description: With the last major firing of its engine, the Artemis II spacecraft is now on a path that will take it around the moon and back.
item.content:encoded:
With the last major firing of its engine, the Artemis II spacecraft is now on a path that will take it around the moon and back.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: This week in science: How much exercise you need, flu resistance and fortified food
item.description: NPR's science podcast Short Wave brings us stories on food fortification, why some people don't seem to get the flu, and a study on how much vigorous exercise you really need.
item.content:encoded: NPR's science podcast Short Wave brings us stories on food fortification, why some people don't seem to get the flu, and a study on how much vigorous exercise you really need.
item.dc:creator: Emily Kwong

item.title: What's so cool about the far side of the moon? A NASA Artemis II scientist explains
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with NASA's Kelsey Evans Young, the Artemis science flight operations lead, about the rigors of space and the lunar slingshot to get home.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with NASA's Kelsey Evans Young, the Artemis science flight operations lead, about the rigors of space and the lunar slingshot to get home.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel J. Sánchez

item.title: EPA flags microplastics, pharmaceuticals as contaminants in drinking water
item.description: There is public concern about health risks from the chemicals, especially from the Make America Healthy Again movement. The agency's move doesn't in itself guarantee regulation.
item.content:encoded:
There is public concern about health risks from the chemicals, especially from the Make America Healthy Again movement. The agency's move doesn't in itself guarantee regulation.

(Image credit: Justin Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Success! After key milestone, Artemis II astronauts speed off toward the moon
item.description: NASA's Artemis II crew has successfully launched on a mission that will take it around the moon and back to Earth. A key maneuver Thursday night sent them hurtling toward their lunar target.
item.content:encoded:
NASA's Artemis II crew has successfully launched on a mission that will take it around the moon and back to Earth. A key maneuver Thursday night sent them hurtling toward their lunar target.

(Image credit: Jim Watson/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Brendan Byrne

item.title: Scientists' efforts to free whale stuck in the Baltic Sea cause online firestorm
item.description: German scientists face a social media firestorm as they try to save a humpback whale in the Baltic Sea.
item.content:encoded: German scientists face a social media firestorm as they try to save a humpback whale in the Baltic Sea.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz
